seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: Ensure that the keywords "research opportunities" and "collaboration" appear in the second paragraph.
new_constraint: The keyword "opportunity" must be used exclusively in its singular form.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. Ensure that the keywords "research opportunities" and "collaboration" appear in the second paragraph. The keyword "opportunity" must be used exclusively in its singular form.
response:
Subject: Introduction and Interest in Your Research – [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

My name is [Your Name], and I am a [Year, e.g., Junior] student majoring in [Your Major] at [University Name]. I have been following your work regarding [mention a specific topic or paper of theirs], and I am deeply impressed by your contributions to the field of [Field of Study].

I am writing to you because I am eager to gain hands-on experience in this area. I am particularly interested in exploring potential research opportunities within your lab. I believe that my background in [mention a skill or relevant course] would allow me to contribute meaningfully to your ongoing projects, and I would welcome any chance for collaboration.

I would appreciate the chance to discuss your work further and see if there is an opportunity for me to assist with your current initiatives. Please let me know if you have any availability for a brief meeting or a call in the coming weeks.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number, optional]
[Your Phone Number]<turn|>
